expectations.text: Opening does NOT use generic proposal language (Dear, Thank you for your interest, Please find attached)
expectations.evidence: none of the banned patterns appeared

expectations.text: Proposal names a specific RP service tier (Mirror, Map, or Territory)
Proposal includes specific pricing ($-amount) within documented ranges
Uses Voice Liberation framing (not generic 'brand strategy' or 'content marketing')
expectations.evidence: Uses Voice Liberation, Narrative Dysregulation, Generative Translation, Bilingual Dashboard, Zero-Measurement Zone throughout

expectations.text: Opener names the client's specific narrative gap/pattern (diagnostic), not generic appreciation
expectations.evidence: Quotes 'polished but hollow' and diagnoses 'losing it at the threshold' pattern

expectations.text: Follows the 6-section structure (Opening, Understanding, Proposed Engagement, Investment, Why This Approach, Next Steps)
expectations.evidence: All six sections present with exact labels

expectations.text: Names concrete deliverables (e.g. Bilingual Dashboard, facilitation sessions, specific asset formats)
expectations.evidence: Bilingual Dashboard, three 90-min sessions, recordings, voice-note support

expectations.text: Closes with ONE clear next step (not a menu)
expectations.evidence: 'Reply to confirm' is the single action

expectations.text: Names Mirror tier specifically (not Map or Territory)
References Ama's specific gap ('polished but hollow', language that doesn't sound like her)
expectations.evidence: Direct quote of full 'polished but hollow' statement plus diagnosis
